text: The patient was given general anesthesia with a single lumen and tracheal tube and placed in the left lateral decubitus position.
After preparing the chest, a 5-millimeter thoracoscopic trocar was introduced into the thoracic cavity in the midaxillary line, fifth intercostal space.
The chest was insufflated to collapse the right lung and parenchyma.
Second trocar was placed in the anterior axillary line, seventh intercostal space.
And to complete the triangulation of the ports, a third trocar was inserted in the posterior axillary line, seventh intercostal space as well.
A vessel-sealing scalpel was then used to open the pericardium.
An appropriately large pericardial window was created with a combination of a vessel-sealing scalpel and endoscopic scissors.
The fluid drained from the pericardium was suctioned from the chest as well as the open pericardial sac.
The procedure was finished with the placement of a chest tube.
